product_goal: Create a low-profile temporary parking number plate for car dashboards that can show a phone number clearly and hide it quickly for privacy without relying on electronics.

hard_constraints.rule: Keep the main body close to 125 x 42 x 14 mm.
Provide a one-handed hide/reveal mechanism for the phone number.
Avoid electronics in the baseline and account for sunlight and cabin heat.

component_envelopes.name: silicone anti-slip pad and ballast zone

reference_cases.lesson: A passive slider can hide digits without requiring electronics.
reference_cases.design_implication: Reserve a shallow front track and finger tab clearance.
reference_cases.source_note: Common marketplace category; verify current suppliers in a live run.

reference_cases.lesson: A hinge makes privacy action obvious but increases side thickness.
reference_cases.design_implication: Use only if the product can tolerate a thicker side profile.

reference_cases.lesson: Night readability improves but battery, service, and heat risks increase.
reference_cases.design_implication: Reserve electronics only if illumination is a hard requirement.
reference_cases.source_note: Active-display product category; current parts not verified in this static example.

component_requirements.design_implication: Front face needs a precise number window.

component_requirements.requirement: Hide or reveal digits in one motion.
component_requirements.design_implication: Body must preserve a slider track or hinge zone.

component_requirements.requirement: Stay stable on dashboard without visible permanent adhesive.
component_requirements.design_implication: Bottom footprint cannot be visually minimized too aggressively.

component_candidates.interface_or_mounting: Mechanical tray behind a front window
component_candidates.visible_design_impact: Thin passive body with precise digit window
component_candidates.risk: Supplier thickness and print durability vary

component_candidates.interface_or_mounting: Top or front track with stop features
component_candidates.visible_design_impact: Clean horizontal gesture and low side profile

component_candidates.candidate: silicone anti-slip pad plus weighted plate
component_candidates.interface_or_mounting: Captured bottom pad and internal ballast
component_candidates.visible_design_impact: Stable broad stance and soft underside detail
component_candidates.risk: Heat aging and durometer are unverified

selected_components.fixed_constraints: Requires a 92 x 18 mm number window and tray retention.
selected_components.unverified: Exact card supplier thickness and print durability.

selected_components.why: Thin one-handed privacy action with clear horizontal product language.
selected_components.fixed_constraints: Requires shallow track, stop features, tab clearance, and anti-rattle detail.
selected_components.unverified: Friction and rattle after heat exposure.

selected_components.selection: silicone anti-slip pad plus internal weighted plate
selected_components.why: Dashboard-safe and passive without exposed permanent adhesive.
selected_components.fixed_constraints: Requires broad bottom contact and captured pad edge.

packaging_constraints: Target total body thickness should remain near 12-14 mm after card, slider, shell, and base stack-up.
Preserve a front number window around 92 x 18 mm with small reveal margin.
Slider track needs end stops, finger tab clearance, and anti-rattle features.
Bottom contact footprint must remain broad enough for dashboard stability.
Avoid active electronics unless illumination becomes a hard requirement.
sourcing_risks: Digit card dimensions vary by supplier.
Silicone pad performance under parked-car heat is unverified.
Current availability, price, and exact supplier dimensions were not verified in this static example.

mounting_strategy.contact_points: broad silicone anti-slip pad with internal weighted plate
mounting_strategy.support_logic: Use bottom footprint and low center of mass instead of permanent adhesive.
mounting_strategy.service_access_notes: Digit cards should be replaceable without opening the whole shell.

style_features.name: low horizontal rail
style_features.rule: Preserve a calm linear silhouette that reflects the slider mechanism.

style_features.name: precise number window
style_features.rule: Keep digits centered and readable, not decorative.

style_features.name: soft stable base
style_features.rule: Let the underside read stable without becoming bulky.

manufacturing_risks: Slider tolerance can create rattle or binding.
Thin cover may warp under cabin heat.
Silicone pad creep can reduce stability.
Exact supplier dimensions are not locked.
forbidden_features: Fake vents or decorative tech details
Tiny digit window that reduces readability
Unverified battery or LED stack in the baseline
Narrow base that compromises dashboard stability

acceptance_checks.title: Readable number window
acceptance_checks.method: Review front elevation and measure window.
acceptance_checks.pass_condition: Digit area is near 92 x 18 mm and remains visually centered.

acceptance_checks.title: Privacy slider package
acceptance_checks.method: Review section stack and motion path.
acceptance_checks.pass_condition: Slider clears number window and has stop/tab space within target body thickness.

acceptance_checks.title: Dashboard stability
acceptance_checks.method: Review bottom footprint and ballast zone.
acceptance_checks.pass_condition: Base contact remains broad and low enough for stable placement.

acceptance_checks.title: Component assumptions
acceptance_checks.method: Check selected component envelopes before CAD.
acceptance_checks.pass_condition: Digit card, slider, and pad dimensions are verified or fallback envelopes are used.
